name: Country Ham and Hominy Bread x Two
recipeInstructions:
---------------------FILLING FOR DOUGH---------------------
1/2 cup Country ham, minced and fried until crisp
1 large onion, diced (1/4-inch)
1/3 cup Chile pulp (Anaheim)
6 tablespoons Molasses
1/4 cup Vegetable Oil
1 1/2 teaspoons Salt
1 Egg, lightly beaten
1 can Yellow Hominy (14 oz.)
2 tablespoons Oat Bran (dust on top)
seenote: Dried Chiles - Reconstitute 6 to 8 Anaheim or Numex Peppers. Scrape flesh from inside the skin and discard the skin. Mash the pulp until smooth using a sturdy fork. Any other mashing method is acceptable.
seenote: Hominy - Drain 14 oz. can of hominy, rinse slightly and chop into small pieces.
Lightly grease a baking sheet with cornmeal. Set aside.
Chop Ham and Onion separately into small dice or large mince. Fry the ham in a dry hot pan until crisp edges form. Remove ham from the pan.
Add onions to the same pan, stir over medium heat for 8-10 minutes. Cook the onions until golden brown but not crisp. Be careful with stirring at this step; avoid crisping.
Combine onions, ham, and hominy and mix well. Let cool.
In a 4-cup measuring cup, stir honey into water and sprinkle yeast on top. Stir until dissolved. Set aside.
Use the dough hook for this project. In a heavy-duty stand mixer bowl, combine bread flour, 1 cup cornmeal, whole wheat flour, molasses, vegetable oil, salt, and egg. Mix on low speed for 1 minute. Add water/honey/yeast mix, onions, chopped hominy, ham, chili pulp, and enough remaining flour to form a soft dough. Mix on low speed for 2 minutes. Knead on low speed, adding additional flour as needed, until the dough is smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky.
Cover the mixing bowl and let rise doubled (1-2 hours).
I usually freeze half of this dough for later use. Divide dough into two balls, freeze in a non-stick sprayed gallon plastic bag, and continue with remaining dough.
Punch down the dough on a lightly floured surface. Roll dough into a 14-inch circle, fold it in half (overlapping slightly off-center), making 4-5 equally spaced cuts from curved edge to folded edge about two-thirds across the loaf (cutting through both layers). Separate the cuts slightly so the loaf opens up and is crescent-shaped. Place on prepared baking sheet and let rise until doubled in volume, about 45 minutes.
Preheat oven to 375°F. Let rise in a warm room for 45 minutes, then place in the oven at 375°F for another 45 minutes or until nicely browned on top and internal temperature reaches 200°F.
Remember this bread is fairly wet; use a flour sifter/duster to lightly flour the dough if it seems too sticky. Brush loaf with melted butter and sprinkle remaining oat bran over the loaf. Bake for 40 minutes, removing from wire rack and allowing to cool completely.
